# MM Personal Sequencer — Visual & UX Redesign Brief
**Generated by Claude Code (EIC) | May 2026**
**Corrective pass on completed build. Do NOT rebuild from scratch.**
**Target files only. All hooks and types are correct — do not touch them.**

---

## Files to Modify

```
components/sequencer/StepCell.tsx
components/sequencer/SequencerGrid.tsx
components/sequencer/SequencerControls.tsx
app/sequencer/page.tsx
```

All other files: do not touch.

---

## Fix 1 — SequencerControls: Remove duplicate Loop control

**Problem:** Loop appears twice — as a `<Button>` in the transport row AND as a `<Switch>` at the bottom of the component. Two controls for one boolean.

**Fix:** Delete the entire `<div className="ml-auto inline-flex items-center gap-2">` block at the bottom of the return (lines containing the Loop Switch and its label). Keep only the Loop `<Button>` in the transport row.

---

## Fix 2 — StepCell: Make node color the primary visual element

**Problem:** Node color is applied at `22` hex alpha (13% opacity) background and `aa` hex alpha border. This is nearly invisible. A step with ROOT assigned is visually indistinguishable from an empty step at a glance.

**Fix:** Replace the current style logic entirely. Use these visual states:

```tsx
// Replace the className and style on the main button element:

<button
  ref={triggerRef as unknown as RefObject<HTMLButtonElement>}
  type="button"
  onClick={onToggle}
  className={cn(
    'relative flex h-[72px] w-full flex-col justify-between rounded-lg border-2 px-2.5 py-2 text-left transition-all',
    'focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-white/40',
    // Rest (inactive): dashed border, very dim
    !step.active && 'border-dashed border-black/15 dark:border-white/15 opacity-40 bg-transparent',
    // Active, no node assigned: solid neutral border, prompt to assign
    step.active && !node && 'border-black/30 dark:border-white/30 bg-black/5 dark:bg-white/5',
    // Active with node: strong color presence
    step.active && node && 'border-transparent',
    // Currently playing: ring
    isCurrentStep && 'ring-2 ring-white ring-offset-1 ring-offset-black/20'
  )}
  style={
    step.active && node
      ? {
          backgroundColor: `${NODE_COLORS[node.index]}33`,   // 20% opacity fill
          borderColor: NODE_COLORS[node.index],               // 100% opacity border
          boxShadow: `0 0 0 1px ${NODE_COLORS[node.index]}66 inset`,
        }
      : undefined
  }
>
```

**Also update the cell content layout:**

```tsx
{/* Top: planet name — PRIMARY identity, large */}
<div
  className="text-sm font-bold leading-tight truncate"
  style={step.active && node ? { color: NODE_COLORS[node.index] } : undefined}
>
  {node ? node.planet : step.active ? '—' : '·'}
</div>

{/* Bottom row: node title + duration badge */}
<div className="flex items-end justify-between">
  <div className="text-[9px] uppercase tracking-widest opacity-60 leading-tight">
    {step.active ? (node ? node.title : 'unassigned') : 'rest'}
  </div>
  <button
    type="button"
    className="rounded bg-black/15 dark:bg-white/15 px-1.5 py-0.5 text-[10px] font-mono"
    onClick={(e) => {
      e.stopPropagation()
      onSetDuration(nextDuration(step.durationMultiplier))
    }}
  >
    ×{step.durationMultiplier}
  </button>
</div>
```

---

## Fix 3 — SequencerGrid: Add empty state and interaction hint

**Problem:** When no steps are active, the user sees 16 identical grey "Rest" boxes with no guidance.

**Fix:** In `SequencerGrid`, add a computed check for whether ANY step is active:

```tsx
const hasActiveSteps = steps.some((s) => s.active)
```

Below the step grid `inline-flex` container, add:

```tsx
{!hasActiveSteps && (
  <div className="mt-3 flex items-center gap-2 text-xs text-gray-400 dark:text-gray-500">
    <span className="rounded border border-dashed border-current px-2 py-0.5 font-mono text-[10px]">
      click
    </span>
    <span>to activate a step</span>
    <span className="mx-1 opacity-40">·</span>
    <span className="rounded border border-dashed border-current px-2 py-0.5 font-mono text-[10px]">
      right-click
    </span>
    <span>to assign a node</span>
  </div>
)}
```

---

## Fix 4 — Page layout: Give the grid visual dominance

**Problem:** Four equal-weight Cards stacked. No hierarchy. The grid card and the controls card look identical in weight.

**Fix:** Replace the page layout in `app/sequencer/page.tsx`.

Remove the wrapping `<Card>` from the grid section. Instead give it direct visual treatment. Change the controls to sit in a compact single-card footer. Full replacement of the return body:

```tsx
return (
  <div className="flex h-full min-h-screen w-full flex-col bg-neutral-50 dark:bg-neutral-950">
    
    {/* Header bar — full width, slim */}
    <div className="border-b border-black/10 dark:border-white/10 bg-white/80 dark:bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-sm px-4 py-3 sm:px-6">
      <SequencerHeader
        title={sequencer.sequence.title}
        isDirty={sequencer.isDirty}
        sequences={sequences}
        onTitleChange={sequencer.setTitle}
        onSave={() => void saveSequence(sequencer.sequence).then(() => sequencer.setIsDirty(false))}
        onLoad={sequencer.loadSequence}
        onNew={() => {
          audio.stop()
          sequencer.resetToNew(user.uid)
        }}
        onDelete={(id) => void deleteSequence(id)}
      />
    </div>

    {/* Grid — hero section, takes available space */}
    <div className="flex-1 overflow-x-auto px-4 pt-6 pb-2 sm:px-6">
      <SequencerGrid
        steps={sequencer.sequence.steps}
        currentStepIndex={audio.currentStepIndex}
        syllableByStepId={syllableByStepId}
        onToggleStep={sequencer.toggleStep}
        onAssignNode={sequencer.assignNode}
        onSetDuration={sequencer.setDuration}
      />
      <div className="mt-1">
        <SyllabicAligner
          steps={sequencer.sequence.steps}
          syllables={sequencer.sequence.syllables}
          currentStepIndex={audio.currentStepIndex}
          overflowSyllables={overflowSyllables}
        />
      </div>
    </div>

    {/* Mantra input — collapsible feel, natural weight */}
    <div className="border-t border-black/10 dark:border-white/10 px-4 py-4 sm:px-6">
      <MantraInput
        mantraText={sequencer.sequence.mantraText}
        mantraLanguage={sequencer.sequence.mantraLanguage}
        ipaText={sequencer.sequence.ipaText}
        syllableCount={sequencer.syllables.length}
        activeStepCount={sequencer.sequence.steps.filter((s) => s.active).length}
        overflow={sequencer.overflow}
        onMantraChange={sequencer.setMantraText}
        onLanguageChange={sequencer.setMantraLanguage}
        onIpaChange={sequencer.setIpaText}
      />
    </div>

    {/* Controls — compact footer bar */}
    <div className="border-t border-black/10 dark:border-white/10 bg-white/80 dark:bg-black/60 backdrop-blur-sm px-4 py-3 sm:px-6">
      <SequencerControls
        isPlaying={audio.isPlaying}
        bpm={sequencer.sequence.bpm}
        stepCount={sequencer.sequence.stepCount}
        loop={sequencer.sequence.loop}
        toneMode={toneMode ?? 'drone'}
        onPlay={() => void audio.play()}
        onPause={audio.pause}
        onStop={audio.stop}
        onBpmChange={sequencer.setBpm}
        onStepCountChange={sequencer.setStepCount}
        onLoopChange={sequencer.setLoop}
        onToneModeChange={setToneMode}
      />
    </div>

  </div>
)
```

**Also update the outer `<div>` of `SequencerControls`** to use a horizontal compact layout instead of `space-y-4`:

```tsx
// Replace the outer div and layout in SequencerControls to:
<div className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-4">
  
  {/* Transport */}
  <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
    <Button type="button" size="icon" variant={isPlaying ? 'default' : 'outline'} onClick={isPlaying ? onPause : onPlay}>
      {isPlaying ? <Pause className="h-4 w-4" /> : <Play className="h-4 w-4" />}
    </Button>
    <Button type="button" size="icon" variant="outline" onClick={onStop}>
      <Square className="h-4 w-4" />
    </Button>
    <Button
      type="button"
      size="sm"
      variant={loop ? 'default' : 'outline'}
      onClick={() => onLoopChange(!loop)}
      className="gap-1"
    >
      <Repeat className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />
      Loop
    </Button>
  </div>

  {/* Divider */}
  <div className="h-6 w-px bg-black/15 dark:bg-white/15" />

  {/* BPM */}
  <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
    <Button type="button" size="sm" variant="outline" className="h-7 px-2 text-xs" onClick={() => onBpmChange(bpm - 5)}>−5</Button>
    <div className="flex min-w-[80px] items-center gap-1.5">
      <Slider
        value={[bpm]}
        min={40}
        max={180}
        step={1}
        onValueChange={([v]) => onBpmChange(v)}
        className="w-20"
      />
      <span className="text-xs tabular-nums text-gray-500">{bpm} bpm</span>
    </div>
    <Button type="button" size="sm" variant="outline" className="h-7 px-2 text-xs" onClick={() => onBpmChange(bpm + 5)}>+5</Button>
  </div>

  {/* Divider */}
  <div className="h-6 w-px bg-black/15 dark:bg-white/15" />

  {/* Steps */}
  <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
    <span className="text-xs text-gray-500">Steps</span>
    {([8, 16, 32] as StepCount[]).map((count) => (
      <Button
        key={count}
        type="button"
        size="sm"
        variant={stepCount === count ? 'default' : 'outline'}
        className="h-7 px-2.5 text-xs"
        onClick={() => onStepCountChange(count)}
      >
        {count}
      </Button>
    ))}
  </div>

  {/* Divider */}
  <div className="h-6 w-px bg-black/15 dark:bg-white/15" />

  {/* Source */}
  <div className="flex items-center gap-1.5">
    <span className="text-xs text-gray-500">Source</span>
    <Button type="button" size="sm" variant={toneMode === 'drone' ? 'default' : 'outline'} className="h-7 px-2.5 text-xs" onClick={() => onToneModeChange('drone')}>
      Drone
    </Button>
    <Button type="button" size="sm" variant={toneMode === 'synthetic' ? 'default' : 'outline'} className="h-7 px-2.5 text-xs" onClick={() => onToneModeChange('synthetic')}>
      Sine
    </Button>
  </div>

</div>
```

Remove the `Switch` import from SequencerControls since it is no longer used after removing the duplicate loop toggle.

---

## NODE_COLORS — Corrected palette

The current colors in `NodePicker.tsx` are generic. Replace them with the following values which better reflect the planetary/elemental character of each node. These are the only values to change in NodePicker.tsx — leave everything else untouched.

```typescript
const NODE_COLORS: Record<NodeIndex, string> = {
  0: '#8b7355',   // ROOT — Saturn — deep warm ochre/earth
  1: '#5b8fa8',   // SACRAL — Neptune — deep teal-blue
  2: '#c0392b',   // SOLAR PLEXUS — Mars — deep red-ochre
  3: '#4a9e72',   // HEART — Venus — deep green
  4: '#c4a35a',   // THROAT — Mercury — burnished gold
  5: '#6b5b9e',   // THIRD EYE — Uranus — deep indigo
  6: '#b8860b',   // MALE CROWN — Jupiter F♯ — dark gold
  7: '#9c6b8a',   // FEMALE CROWN — Jupiter A♯ — deep rose-mauve
  8: '#4a7fa5',   // ETHERAL HEART — Earth — deep ocean blue
}
```

These are muted, grounded tones — not saturated. They carry planetary character without clashing with the neutral MM aesthetic.

---

## What the result should look like

- Header bar: slim, title + save + sequence picker in one horizontal strip at the top
- Grid: the dominant element, takes most of the vertical space, steps clearly distinguishable by node color at a glance
- Active steps with nodes: strong color border + 20% tinted fill + planet name prominent in node color
- Rest steps: nearly invisible — dim, dashed, silent
- Syllabic aligner: tight strip immediately below the grid, syllables aligned visually to their steps
- Mantra input: below the grid section, unobtrusive
- Controls: compact single-row footer bar — transport + BPM + steps + source in one horizontal strip
- Empty state hint visible when no steps are active
- The whole page should feel like an instrument, not a settings panel

---
